title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE CITY OF ILIGAN FROM THE FIRST LEGISLATIVE DISTRICT OF THE PROVINCE OF LANAO DEL NORTE TO CONSTITUTE THE LONE LEGISLATIVE DISTRICT OF THE CITY OF ILIGAN

chunks.text:
H. No. 4054; Standard Today, Manila Times, October 22, 2009

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9724, October 20, 2009 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE CITY OF ILIGAN FROM THE FIRST LEGISLATIVE DISTRICT OF THE PROVINCE OF LANAO DEL NORTE TO CONSTITUTE THE LONE LEGISLATIVE DISTRICT OF THE CITY OF ILIGAN

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representative of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Lone Legislative District.

ГўВЂВ” The City of Iligan is hereby separated from the First Legislative District of the Province of Lanao del Norte to constitute the Lone Legislative District of the City of Iligan, which shall commence in the next national election after the effectivity of this Act.

SEC. 2.

Incumbent Representative.

ГўВЂВ” The incumbent Representative of the First Legislative District of the Province of Lanao del Norte shall continue to represent the said district until the new Representative of the Lone Legislative District of the City of Iligan shall have been elected and qualified.

SEC. 3.

Rules and Regulations.

ГўВЂВ” The Commission on Elections shall issue the necessary rules and regulations to implement this Act.

SEC. 3.

Rules and Regulations.

ГўВЂВ” The Commission on Elections shall issue the necessary rules and regulations to implement this Act.

SEC. 4.

Repealing Clause.

ГўВЂВ” All laws, rules and regulations inconsistent with this Act are hereby repealed, amended or modified accordingly.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

ГўВЂВ” This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in at least two (2) newspapers of general circulation.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

(Sgd.) PROSPERO C. NOGRALES

President of Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on May 28, 2008 and September 28, 2009, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary of Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: OCT 20 2009

(Sgd.) GLORIA MACAPAGAL - ARROYO

President of the Philippines
